title: acaena pterocarpa
intro: Acaena pterocarpa, also known as the sheep's burr or variegated buttonbush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in New Zealand, where it is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Acaena pterocarpa include treating minor wounds, reducing swelling, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in tinctures or ointments for topical application.
